name: Universal Shorts Pattern Database
description: Research-backed patterns for high-performing YouTube Shorts across all genres
sources: Opus.pro: YouTube Shorts Caption Best Practices
Aibrify: YouTube Shorts Retention Curve Playbook

ideal_length.reason: Sweet spot for completion + rewatch without viewer fatigue

timing.reason: Captions must appear immediately to establish hook

visual_change_interval.reason: Visual changes keep viewers engaged; longer than 4s causes drop-off

hook_window.reason: First 3 seconds are critical for hook; viewer decision point

timing.reason: 3-4 audio peaks throughout keeps energy up without exhaustion

hooks.pattern: Sudden loud sound, music drop, or audio spike
hooks.example: Gunshot, record scratch, bass drop, scream

hooks.pattern: Rapid zoom, scene change, flash, or motion burst
hooks.example: Jump scare, quick zoom to face, scene cut

hooks.pattern: Strong facial expression, emotion, or body language
hooks.example: Shock face, jaw drop, excited jump

hooks.pattern: Text caption that creates curiosity or urgency
hooks.example: WAIT FOR THIS, THEN IT HAPPENED, YOU WON'T BELIEVE
hooks.impact: Cognitive hook - viewer wants to know what happens

hooks.pattern: Instant kill, clutch play, or unexpected outcome

safe_zone_margins.reason: Avoid mobile safe area cutoffs (notches, home bar)

caption_strategy.hook_phase: Setup or intrigue (WAIT FOR THIS, WATCH WHAT HAPPENS)
caption_strategy.middle_phase: Context or narrative (THIS IS INSANE, I COULDN'T BELIEVE IT)
caption_strategy.payoff_phase: Reaction or climax (TOLD YOU SO, BEST MOMENT EVER)
caption_strategy.ending_phase: Loop hook or call-to-action (WAIT TIL THE END, REWATCH THIS)

retention.reason: Loop-friendly endings increase rewatch rate by 34%
Silence without visuals kills 35% of completion rate
Frequent visual changes boost completion by 22%
retention.no_dead_air_rule: Always have either audio or visual change happening

hook_phase.must_have: At least one of: audio shock, visual shock, reaction

motion_and_pacing.reason: More cuts = higher perceived energy; too many = dizzying
motion_and_pacing.momentum_escalation: Gradually increase cut frequency through video

audio.music_alignment: Sync cuts and visuals to beat drops

circular_narrative.description: Ending loops back to beginning for rewatches
circular_narrative.example: Start: 'I FOUND A GLITCH' → End: 'THERE'S THE GLITCH AGAIN'

cliffhanger.description: Ending creates curiosity for next video
cliffhanger.example: End on mystery that continues in next Shorts

call_to_action.description: Explicit ask to subscribe, follow, or rewatch
call_to_action.example: REWATCH THIS and catch everything you missed

novelty_scoring.definition: Fresh, unexpected, or original content elements
factors: Unique gameplay clip (no one else has this footage)
novelty_scoring.reason: Viewers stay longer for content they haven't seen before

safe_zones.reason: Ensure text is readable on all phones without clipping
